title: Apparatuses and methods for improving post-operative recovery from surgery
abstract: This disclosure relates to apparatuses and methods for preventing the onset and progression of POI. In one example, a fan-like polyurethane heat-sealed bilayer that surrounds a plurality of wedge-shaped foam strips that join at a collecting foam portion, is subjected to negative pressure provided through silicone tubing which is sealed to the perforated collecting foam portion. Such negative pressure applied for approximately 48 to 72 hours after closure of the abdomen, helps prevent POI which in turn enhances patient recovery, and reduces the length of their hospital stay.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus for improving post-operative recovery from surgery comprising: a plurality of biocompatible foam strips; and a bilayer encompassing the plurality of strips. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of offset perforations distributed throughout the plurality of foam strips and the bilayer. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of creases parallel to the plurality of foam strips. 4. The apparatus of claim 1 further comprising a connecting sponge region. 5. The apparatus of claim 1 further comprising a silicone -based connecting tube. 6. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the bilayer and foam strips are fluidly connected. 7. The apparatus of claim 6, further comprising a negative pressure means that is in fluid communication with the bilayer encompassing the plurality of strips. 8. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the bilayer is selected from the group consisting of thermoplastic polyethylene and thermoplastic polyurethane. 9. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the foam is selected from the group consisting of polyurethane (PU), polyethylene (PE) and polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) foam. 10. A method for preventing the onset and progression of POI comprising the steps of: placing a plurality of foam strips enveloped in a bilayer on the bowels; and applying negative vacuum pressure therapy to the plurality of strips. 11. The method of claim 10 further comprising the step of: removing the plurality of foam strips enveloped in the bilayer by retraction from an incision of diameter no larger than approximately 2 cm in a patient. 12. An apparatus for decreasing post-operative infections or hematoma comprising a bilayer drape for removing blood and fluid in the post-operative abdominal cavity, wherein the drape is fluidly connected to a negative pressure delivery means. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the bilayer drape comprises: a plurality of biocompatible foam strips; and at least two sheets of thermoplastic polyurethane encompassing the plurality of strips. 14. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the drape further comprises a first outer surface in communication with a tissue in a patient. 15. The apparatus of claim 13, further comprising a plurality of offset perforations distributed throughout the plurality of foam strips inside the bilayer drape. 16. The apparatus of claim 13, further comprising a plurality of creases parallel to the plurality of foam strips. 17. The apparatus of claim 13 further comprising a connecting sponge region. 18. The apparatus of claim 13 further comprising a silicone -based connecting tube. 19. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the at least two sheets of thermoplastic polyurethane and plurality of foam strips are fluidly connected. 20. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the negative pressure delivery means is a vacuum pump with a regulator.
